decisions.briefing: Markets are strongly bullish today with NASDAQ up 1.7% and S&P 500 up 1.1% in a clear risk-on environment. However, our cash sits at $477 — just below the $500 threshold needed to open new positions — so we're holding rather than forcing a thin buy. All six existing positions remain active with auto stop-loss and profit-target mechanisms in place to manage exits.

market_education.summary_en: Equities staged a broad risk-on rally today, with the NASDAQ surging 1.7% and the S&P 500 gaining 1.1%, driven by renewed investor appetite for growth assets as macro fears continued to ease. Technology and semiconductor names led the charge, reflecting improving sentiment around AI infrastructure spending and a softer rate outlook. The session underscores how quickly capital rotates back into high-beta names once uncertainty subsides.
market_education.summary_zh: 今日股市掀起全面風險偏好行情，那斯達克指數強勁上漲1.7%，標普500指數亦上揚1.1%。投資者重拾對成長型資產的興趣，宏觀不確定性持續降溫，科技及半導體板塊領漲。人工智能基礎建設支出前景樂觀，加之市場對聯儲局利率政策的預期趨於溫和，進一步提振市場情緒。此輪升勢再次印證，一旦不確定因素消散，資金便會迅速回流高貝塔值股票。

daily_lesson.explanation_en: Cash drag is the performance cost of holding idle cash in a rising market — your uninvested dollars earn little while stocks climb. Today is a perfect example: we have 4 open portfolio slots and a basket of high-momentum candidates scoring 95, yet $477 in cash sits just below the $500 buy threshold, unable to participate in a 1.7% NASDAQ rally. Managing the cash-vs-deployment balance is a core active portfolio skill.
daily_lesson.explanation_zh: 「現金拖累」（Cash Drag）是指在市場上漲期間，持有閒置現金所造成的機會成本。今日便是典型案例：組合尚有四個空置倉位，且選股器篩出多隻動能評分高達95的強勢候選股，但帳戶現金僅477美元，略低於500美元的建倉門檻，無法把握那斯達克1.7%的升幅。如何在持現與部署資金之間取得平衡，正是主動管理組合的核心技巧之一。
